“The Customs of Cambodia”: a cornerstone for Angkor For Cambodian history, Pelliot’s most decisive contribution is his work on Zhou Daguan’s 13th‑century account, often referred to in English as “The Customs of Cambodia.” This Chinese text is the only detailed eyewitness description of Angkor at its political and architectural peak. Without it, historians would have inscriptions, art and  ...    Pelliot produced the first rigorous modern edition and French translation of Zhou Daguan, combining philological  ...    but for Cambodia and Southeast Asia his legacy is more subtle and perhaps more important. title: Zhou Daguan
highlights:
diplomate chinois du temps de l'empereur Témur Khan (Chengzong), principalement connu par le récit d'une de ses missions, intitulé Mémoires sur les coutumes du Cambodge.  ...    ## Les Mémoires sur les coutumes du Cambodge De son nom chinois 真臘風土記, hanyu pinyin Zhēnlà fēngtǔ jì, ce récit rapporte de façon détaillée la vie et les coutumes des habitants du Cambodge (on remarque que le chinois utilise l'ancien nom de Chenla).
